signals.headline: South Korea March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2.16-2.2%** | YES | HIGH confidence

South Korea's **annual inflation rate rose to 2.16-2.2% in March 2026**, up from 2.0% in both January and February.[1][2] This represents a **5-month high** and exceeded market forecasts of 2.4%.[2] Month-over-month, consumer prices increased 0.34% from February to March.[1] The increase was driven by price pressure
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Germany March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2
signals.raw:
Germany March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2.7%** | YES | HIGH confidence

Germany's **annual inflation rate climbed to 2.7% in March 2026**, confirmed from preliminary estimates, accelerating sharply from 1.9% in February.[3] This marks the **highest level since January 2024**, driven by a sharp rebound in energy prices which surged 7.2%, with fuel costs up 20% and light heating oil up 44.4%.[3]
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Market Implications:** South Korea's inflation remains near the Bank of Korea's 2% target with contained price pressures
signals.raw: Market Implications:** South Korea's inflation remains near the Bank of Korea's 2% target with contained price pressures, while Germany's spike reflects renewed global energy volatility. These developments would likely influence monetary policy expectations and energy-sensitive sector positioning in prediction markets.
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?
